analyst_finding: IDENTITY MAPPING VERIFIED — W004 in results.json IS the eclipse test, NOT a category error

wins_page_w004.note: The dome wins page WIN-004 refers to Noise Floor Limit (empirical recalibration, HUA lunar transit baseline) — a DIFFERENT item.
wins_page_w004.implication: results.json uses its own W-numbering scheme (W001, W004, TASK-3-1) that is NOT the same as the wins-page WIN numbering. W004 in results.json = our WIN-025 (eclipse test), NOT wins-page WIN-004 (Noise Floor Limit).

conclusion.reasoning: SEC-2b.7 states that W004 in results.json is the 2024 Eclipse 9-Station test. This is confirmed by the entry content: prediction -10±2 nT, observation -17.6 nT, confirmed_strong. The same test is marked [REMOVED] on the wins tab due to storm contamination. The two-scoreboard contradiction (confirmed_strong vs REMOVED for the same observation) is real and correctly documented.
conclusion.notation_improvement: SEC-2b.7 notation "W004 / WIN-025" could confuse readers who look up W004 on the dome wins page (where it refers to a different item). Recommend clarifying: "results.json entry W004 (the dome's internal API label for the 2024 Eclipse 9-Station Replication, corresponding to our WIN-025)" to make the cross-reference unambiguous.
conclusion.severity_downgrade: From CRITICAL to minor — the factual claim is correct. Only the notation needs a small clarification to avoid namespace confusion between the dome's two numbering systems.

recommended_patch.find: Scoreboard split on the same observation (W004 / WIN-025).
recommended_patch.replace_with: Scoreboard split on the same observation (results.json entry W004, the dome's internal label for the 2024 Eclipse 9-Station Replication — our WIN-025).
